ogTitle: Diamond Head vs Diggers Camp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Diamond Head and Diggers Camp.
heading: Diamond Head vs Diggers Camp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Diamond Head, New South Wales and Diggers Camp, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Diamond Head has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Diggers Camp (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Diamond Head or Diggers Camp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Diamond Head has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Diggers Camp scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Diamond Head scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Diggers Camp scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Diamond Head or Diggers Camp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Diamond Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
